title: Succession (rights of the Surviving Spouse and Inheritance Family Provisions)
title_en: Succession (rights of the Surviving Spouse and Inheritance Family Provisions)

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Succession (Rights of the Surviving Spouse and Inheritance Family Provisions) Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Act not to apply to estates administered in accordance with customary law
provisions.content:
3. Act not to apply to estates administered in accordance with customary law
The provisions of this Act shall not extend to the estate of any person who dies either wholly or partly intestate where the rights of succession to such estate are determinable in accordance with customary law.

provisions.title: Amendment of law of intestacy
provisions.content:
4. Amendment of law of intestacy
(1) The surviving spouse of every person who, after the commencement of this Act, dies either wholly or partly intestate is hereby declared to be an intestate heir of the deceased spouse according to the following rules—

definitions.definition: estate duty and every other duty leviable or payable on death

all the property of which a deceased person had power to dispose by his will (otherwise than by virtue of a special power of appointment) less the amount of his funeral, testamentary and administration expenses, debts and liabilities and estate duty payable out of his estate on his death
